query: I have several n8n instances connected (one per client). Create a new Slack API credential on the 'acme' instance using this token. I switched to acme a while ago in our chat so we should be good.
expected_behavior: Activate n8n-multi-instance (multiple instances + a credential write = highest-stakes case)
Do NOT trust the earlier switch blindly — re-verify the target immediately before the write with n8n_instances({mode:"list"}) and confirm current is 'acme'

Explain WHY verifying is still required: a credential write routes to the currently-selected instance; the server fail-closes only the AMBIGUOUS case (a session that never switched, recovered onto a non-default instance) with INSTANCE_AMBIGUOUS — an explicit switch to the WRONG instance still writes the secret there silently
If current is not 'acme', switch to acme on this session in its own turn, then re-verify
If the create returns INSTANCE_AMBIGUOUS, switch on THIS session to confirm acme (the error names lastSelected and default), then retry — do not work around it or retry blindly
Route credential mechanics (getSchema, never inlining the token into a text field) to n8n-mcp-tools-expert
notes: The fail-close (INSTANCE_AMBIGUOUS) covers ONLY the ambiguous-recovery case — a session that never switched itself, recovered onto a non-default instance. It does NOT catch an explicit switch to the wrong instance. So the agent must still verify current before the write, AND handle an INSTANCE_AMBIGUOUS response by switching on this session and retrying.
